MEMORANDUM OF COSTS AND DISBURSEMENTS, $64,775.00 TOTAL COSTS

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT. PLTF. BERNIE FRUGOLI WAS OF UNSOUND MIND AND LACKED THE MENTAL CAPACITY TO ENTER INTO WRITTEN LEASE EXTENSION AGREEMENTS WITH DEFENDANTS LOWELL THOMAS AND SYBIL W. THOMAS ON 9/20/00. PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CIVIL CODE SECTION 39, THESE LEASE AGREEMENTS ATTACHED HERETO AS EXHIBITS A AND B ARE ORDERED RESCINDED. 2. ON PLAINTIFFS' DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION, THE LEASE EXTENSION AGREEMENTS ATTACHED HERETO AS EXHIBITS "A" AND "B" ARE DECLARED NULL AND VOID AND OF NO FURCE AND EFFECT; 3. ON PLAINTIFF'S DECLATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION THE COURT FINDS THAT THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE OPTION TO PURCHASE AS SET FORTH IN PARAGRAPHS 22 AND 33 OF THE WRITTEN RESIDENTIAL LEASE WITH OPTION TO PURCHASE ENTERED INTO BETWEEN BERNICE FRUGOLI AND DEFENDANTS LOWELL THOMAS, AND SYBIL W. THOMAS A COPY OF WHICH IS ATTACHED AS EXHIBIT C ARE UNCERTAIN, INDEFINITE, AND INCOMPLETE. THEREFORE THE OPTION TO PURCHASE IS UNENFORCEABLE AS A MATTER OF LAW; 4. PLAINTIFFS RECOVER FROM DEFENDANTS LOWELL THOMAS AND SYBIL W. THOMAS AKA CANDI THOAMS, THE SUM OF $62,981.50 IN ATTORNEY FEES AND $1,081.00 AS AND FOR COST TOGETHER WITH INTEREST THEREON SAID JUDGMENT AS PROVIDED BY LAW.
UNCONTESTED CALENDAR OF DEC-16-2002, DEFAULT PROVE-UP IN SCD 218 AT 09:00 AM ON DECEMBER 16, 2002 GRANTED PER WRITTEN ORDER. COMMISSIONER: LORETTA M. NORRIS; CLERK: P. DIAS; REPORTER: LORETTA NAJARA.

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for hearing this day. Commissioner: Loretta M. Norris; Clerk: P. Dias; Reporter: Loretta Najara. Patrick C. Kerwin, Esq. appeared for Plaintiff, Bernice Frugoli. Albert O'Brien and Dr. Abraham Nievod were sworn and examined on behalf of the plaintiff. The following exhibits were marked for identification and moved into evidence: 1) Order appointing Special Administrator; 2) Lease Extension Agreement dated September 20, 2000; 3) Lease Extension Agreement dated September 20, 2002; 4) Residential Lease with option to purchase; 5) Curriculum Vitae of Dr. Abraham Nievod; and 6) Report of Dr.Abraham Nievod. The Court receives evidence and takes the matter under submission.
